summary: The build failed during Meson/CMake preliminary checks because C compile/link tests failed due to missing system headers and related errors.
root_cause: Preliminary Meson/CMake compilation checks failed because required system headers (for example the Python C API header and other C headers) were not found, causing compile errors and linker failures.

evidence.reason: Shows the Python C API header (Python.h) was not found during the build checks.
Indicates the build system aborted early because preliminary compilation checks failed.

evidence.quote: collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
evidence.reason: Shows linker failures occurred as part of the build process, consistent with failed compilation/link steps.
Shows other required C headers were also missing, not just Python headers.

evidence.quote: /home/runner/work/numpy/numpy/build/meson-private/tmpati5_57k/testfile.c:9:32: error: 'isfinite' undeclared (first use in this function)
evidence.reason: Demonstrates compilation errors for standard math/builtin functions, indicating missing definitions or wrong include environment during checks.
Further evidence that essential system headers are not available to the build.

suggested_fix: Ensure the CI environment includes required development headers and build dependencies (for example install the Python development headers and standard C dev headers). On Debian/Ubuntu runners this typically means installing packages such as python3-dev and build-essential (e.g. apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3-dev build-essential) or using a runner image that already provides the Python C headers; then re-run the job.
